Package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.auth

Class BearerTokenAuthFilter

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.rest.server.auth.AuthFilter
org.apache.juneau.rest.server.auth.BearerTokenAuthFilter
All Implemented Interfaces:
jakarta.servlet.Filter, Authenticator
public class BearerTokenAuthFilter extends AuthFilter
AuthFilter that authenticates requests via the RFC 6750 Bearer Token scheme.

On each request this filter:

  1. Reads the Authorization header.
  2. If the header is absent or does not start with "Bearer ", returns Optional.empty() (filter does not apply).
  3. Extracts the raw token string and delegates to the configured TokenValidator.
  4. On success, builds an AuthResult carrying the returned Principal and any roles extracted from a ClaimsPrincipal claim (configurable claim name; default "roles").
  5. On validator failure (AuthenticationException thrown), re-throws with a WWW-Authenticate: Bearer realm="<realm>" challenge.
Usage

AuthFilterChain.create(bs) .append(BearerTokenAuthFilter.create() .pattern("/api/*") .validator(jwtValidator) .build()) .build();

See Also:
  • TokenValidator
  • AuthenticationException
  • AuthFilter
  • RFC 6750 — Bearer Token Usage
  • AuthN Filter Framework
Since:
10.0.0

    • authenticate

      public Optional<AuthResult> authenticate(jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest req) throws AuthenticationException
      Description copied from interface: Authenticator
      Inspects the request and returns the authentication result.
      Parameters:
      req - The incoming HTTP request. Never null.
      Returns:
      The authentication result, or Optional.empty() if this authenticator does not apply.
      Throws:
      AuthenticationException - If the request carries recognizable credentials that are invalid.
